recommendations.problem: Default 1024 MB ephemeral storage insufficient for Playwright browser operations

solution.command:
# Via AWS Console:
# 1. Navigate to CloudWatch console
# 2. Go to Metrics > All metrics
# 3. Search for "EphemeralStorageUsagePercent" in the search bar
# 4. Select CloudWatchSynthetics namespace
# 5. Filter by your canary name to see storage usage percentage
solution.description: Verify EphemeralStorageUsagePercent metric in CloudWatch Synthetics
solution.expected_outcome: Metric shows high ephemeral storage usage during timeout failures

solution.description: Increase ephemeral storage from default 1024 MB to 2048 MB or more
solution.expected_outcome: Canary configuration updated with increased ephemeral storage

solution.description: Verify timeout issues resolve with increased storage
solution.expected_outcome: Canary passes consistently, EphemeralStorageUsagePercent metric decreases

recommendations.title: Increase Lambda ephemeral storage to 2048 MB or higher

recommendations.problem: High memory usage may contribute to slower page loads

solution.description: Higher memory allocation provides more CPU and better performance
solution.expected_outcome: Improved performance, lower memory usage percentage
solution.step: Increase Lambda memory available to the canary while its running

solution.command:
# Via AWS Console:
# 1. Navigate to CloudWatch Synthetics console
# 2. Select your canary
# 3. Select a failed canary run from the canary run availability graph
# 4. Check the canary run execution log under "Logs" tab.
solution.description: Check that Max Memory Used stays well below allocation

recommendations.title: Increase Lambda memory allocation

recommendations.problem: 60-second default timeout may be insufficient for JavaScript-heavy pages

solution.expected_outcome: Page loads successfully within extended timeout

recommendations.title: Increase page load timeout for heavy pages

root_cause: Playwright-based canaries run Chromium inside Lambda, which writes browser cache, downloads, and temp files to /tmp (ephemeral storage). With the default 1024 MB ephemeral storage, Chromium can exhaust available disk space during page load, causing I/O stalls that prevent the page from finishing within the 60-second navigation timeout. The EphemeralStorageUsagePercent metric exceeds 90% during these failures. Higher memory usage (55-60% of allocation) further reduces available CPU time, compounding the slowdown.
symptoms: Canary intermittently fails with page load timeout
Manual testing of same endpoint works fine
Memory usage approaching or exceeding 56% of allocated memory
Playwright navigation times out waiting for 'load' event
title: Playwright page load timeout with high Lambda resource usage
